problem: Stakeholders requested lively animations (count-ups, row cascades, parallax) to make the dashboard feel modern. In a dense, data-heavy interface this risks distraction, motion sickness, and perceived sluggishness.
context: The enterprise-pm-genome motion_character is 'restrained and functional; 120-180ms ease-out, motion only to clarify state change or spatial relationship' and prohibits 'novelty-animation'. Users work in the tool daily for long sessions.
alternatives: Expressive motion system with entrance cascades and count-ups, matching consumer SaaS trends
No motion at all — risks losing useful state-change and spatial cues
Restrained, functional motion: short ease-out transitions only where they clarify state or layering, all gated behind prefers-reduced-motion
decision: Adopt a restrained, functional motion budget: 120-180ms ease-out transitions used only to clarify state change or spatial relationship; no decorative count-ups or cascades; every effect honours prefers-reduced-motion.
rationale: Dense professional UIs reward calm; motion should reduce cognitive load, not add to it. This conforms to the genome motion_character and accessibility posture and keeps long sessions comfortable.
accessibility_impact: Directly satisfies WCAG 2.3.3; all non-essential motion disabled when prefers-reduced-motion is set. Note: effect-kpi-count-up currently violates this and is tracked as motion debt.
performance_impact: Avoids heavy animation libraries on the main route, protecting the JS budget and main-thread time.
implementation: effect-row-hover-highlight and effect-toast-confirmation are governed-by constraint-reduced-motion; durations capped at 180ms.
validation: Audited in the 2026-04 WCAG review; reduced-motion behaviour spot-checked per effect.
